commit.message:
Fix TTL rounding to match Redis behavior (#1602)

* Fix TTL rounding to match Redis behavior (#1389)

Redis rounds TTL seconds to the nearest integer (equivalent to (ms+500)/1000),
while Garnet was truncating (flooring) via a direct (long) cast. This caused
SET+EXPIRE 10+TTL to return 9 instead of 10 when called immediately.

Fix: Use Math.Round(MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero) in both
SecondsFromDiffUtcNowTicks and MillisecondsFromDiffUtcNowTicks in
ConvertUtils.cs.

Added TtlRoundingTest to verify string and object keys return the expected
TTL value immediately after EXPIRE.

[cDAC] Implement GetContext and GetAppDomain on ClrDataFrame (#125064)

## Summary

Implement `GetContext` and `GetAppDomain` on the managed `ClrDataFrame`
(IXCLRDataFrame), replacing the legacy-only delegation with native cDAC
implementations that match the behavior of the native `stack.cpp`
implementations.

## Changes

### New files
- **`ClrDataAppDomain.cs`** — `[GeneratedComClass]` implementing
`IXCLRDataAppDomain` with:
  - `GetName` via the Loader contract's `GetAppDomainFriendlyName()`
- `GetUniqueID` returning `1` (CoreCLR single AppDomain, matching native
`DefaultADID`)
  - `GetFlags` returning `0` (`CLRDATA_DOMAIN_DEFAULT`)
- **`ClrDataFrameDumpTests.cs`** — Dump-based integration tests
organized by IXCLRDataFrame method, each selecting its own debuggee

Improve telemetry error classification with typed sentinels (#7051)

* Eliminate opaque errors_errorString from telemetry

Looked at error telemetry for azd 1.23.7 and 1.23.8. About
15k were just 'internal.errors_errorString' and ~3.6k were
'error.suggestion' — not useful for debugging.

Why this was happening:

- MapError was matching ErrorWithSuggestion wrapper before
  looking at the real error inside, so things like auth and
  ARM failures got misclassified.
- Most Run() methods used bare errors.New or fmt.Errorf with
  no typed error, so MapError had nothing to match on and
  dumped them in the catch-all.

What this does:

- Unwrap ErrorWithSuggestion first so the real error gets
  classified
- Add 28 typed errors covering all commands
- Fix all 54 bare error sites to wrap typed errors with %w
- Add ErrorWithSuggestion at each site for better agent/CLI
  experience
- Add an AST test that blocks new bare errors from being added

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove non-actionable suggestions in extensions.go

Return base errors instead of wrapping with unhelpful
'This is an internal error...' suggestion text.

Addresses JeffreyCA review feedback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert ErrorWithSuggestion unwrap logic

Restore original error.suggestion + error.type classification.
Wei confirmed the empty error.type was a backend GDPR
classification issue, now fixed. The original design is correct:
ErrorWithSuggestion produces error.suggestion as ResultCode
with the inner error type in the error.type attribute.

fix(api): enable manual entry for values with allowed ranges (#4588) Z-Wave JS v15.21.0 changed the value metadata format for values like `wakeUpInterval` to include both `states` (named options: "Default", "Disabled") and `allowed` (range entries: `{ from: 300, to: 86400, step: 60 }`). The UI rendered these as a simple dropdown with no manual entry because `allowManualEntry` was undefined for non-Configuration CC values. ### Changes - In `_updateValueMetadata`, when processing values with `states`, check if `allowManualEntry` is undefined and `allowed` contains range entries (objects with `from`/`to`) - If so, default `allowManualEntry` to `true`, enabling the combobox UI that allows both dropdown selection and custom value entry ```typescript if ( valueId.allowManualEntry === undefined && valueId.allowed?.some((entry) => 'from' in entry && 'to' in entry) ) { valueId.allowManualEntry = true } ``` This restores the ability to enter custom wake up intervals (and similar values) within the allowed range while preserving the named preset options.
Original prompt > > ---- > > *This section details on the original issue you should resolve* > > Unable to change wakeUpInterval value to anything except Default or Disabled > ### Checklist > > - [x] I am not using Home Assistant. Or: a developer has told me to come here. > - [x] I have checked the troubleshooting section and my problem is not described there. > - [x] I have read the changelog and my problem is not mentioned there. > > ### Deploy method > > NPM > > ### Z-Wave JS UI version > > 11.14.0 > > ### ZwaveJS version > > 15.21.1 > > ### Describe the bug > > I am unable to change the wake up interval value for a battery device except for Default and Disabled: > > > > ### To Reproduce > > 1. Have a battery device > 2. Make sure it is interviewed on latest version > 3. Try to change wake up interval > > > ### Expected behavior > > For this particular device I should be able to choose any value from 300 to 86,400 in increments of 60 seconds. This is reported in the interview: > > ```text > 2026-03-10 03:48:33.037 CNTRLR « [Node 263] received wakeup capabilities: > default wakeup interval: 43200 seconds > minimum wakeup interval: 300 seconds > maximum wakeup interval: 86400 seconds > wakeup interval steps: 60 seconds > wakeup on demand supported: false > ``` > > This is allowed on similar devices that were interviewed in an earlier version, as the input is a text box: > > > > I assume if I re-interview that device it will also break the ability to change it. > > ### Additional context > > Z-Wave JS v15.21.0 changed the value format for wake up interval, so I guess ZUI no longer understands it. https://github.com/zwave-js/zwave-js/pull/8607 > > This issue affects a device I just included with v15.21.0. It does not affect devices included with an older version of Z-Wave JS. The format was not migrated for existing devices. You can see the difference with these debug files. > > 1. non-working device: [node_263_dump.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25892624/node_263_dump.json) > 2. working device: [node_262_dump.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25892641/node_262_dump.json) > > > > ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section) > > > >

Fix UIKit thread error in AppInfo and DeviceDisplay handlers

AppInfo.Current (RequestedTheme, RequestedLayoutDirection) and
DeviceDisplay.MainDisplayInfo access UIKit APIs that must be called
on the main thread. The HTTP server handlers run on a background thread,
causing 'UIKit Consistency error' on iOS.

Wrap both handlers in MainThread.InvokeOnMainThreadAsync() to dispatch
to the UI thread before accessing these APIs.

Fixes #34

Add Comet gesture tap, IScrollView scroll, and stable ID support

Three changes to support Comet views in MauiDevFlow automation:

1. HandleTap: Add IGestureView support via reflection. Comet views use
   IGestureView.Gestures with TapGesture.Invoke() instead of MAUI
   TapGestureRecognizer. New TryInvokeCometGestureTap checks for the
   interface by name and invokes tap gestures without a Comet dependency.

2. HandleScroll: Add IView/IScrollView support. Comet ScrollView
   implements IScrollView but not Controls.ScrollView. Added
   TryNativeScrollOnHandler for element-targeted scroll, and
   FindDescendantIScrollView for page-level scroll. Platform override
   TryNativeScrollOnPlatformView delegates to native UIScrollView
   (iOS/macCatalyst), RecyclerView (Android), or ScrollViewer (Windows).

3. GenerateId: Extract platform view from IView.Handler for
   EnsurePlatformStableId. Comet auto-stamps AccessibilityIdentifier
   on native views, but GenerateId was passing the Comet.View to
   EnsurePlatformStableId which expected a UIView. Now falls through
   to handler.PlatformView, yielding stable platform-stamped IDs.

Validated against CometControlsGallery on Mac Catalyst:
- Sidebar tap (Text + OnTap gesture): WORKS
- Button tap (IButton interface): WORKS
- ScrollView scroll (element-targeted): WORKS
- Page-level scroll (IScrollView discovery): WORKS
- MAUI reference app: No regressions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[RyuJit Wasm] Fix Signature Generation Bug with Un-called methods (#125090)

#124685 changed our signature recording logic in favor of using
`WasmTypeNode`s in the dependency graph. However, we do not create these
nodes currently unless they are the target of a relocation, but every
compiled method will need one. This PR adds `WasmTypeNode` as a static
dependency of `MethodWithGCInfo` to make sure we always create a type
signature node for every method we compile.

Add BFloat16 support via Core.BFloat16 tangent infrastructure and BFloat16s extension (#1042)

* Add Core.BFloat16 tangent infrastructure and rules

- Tangent space of BFloat16 is BFloat16 itself (mirrors IEEEFloat treatment)
- Implements all tangent/fdata/rdata infrastructure methods
- Conversion rules: BFloat16 <-> Float32/Float64
- Math rules: sqrt, cbrt, exp*, log*, trig, hyperbolic, hypot, ^, atan2, max, min, abs, eps, nextfloat, prevfloat
- All code guarded by `@static if isdefined(Core, :BFloat16)` (Julia >= 1.11)
- Wires into CI test matrix as 'rules/bfloat16'

Closes #935

* Move BFloat16 rules to MooncakeBFloat16sExt

- src/rules/bfloat16.jl now only contains type-level methods
  (tangent_type, fdata_type, rdata_type, can_produce_zero_rdata_from_type, etc.)
  that work without BFloat16s.jl loaded
- ext/MooncakeBFloat16sExt.jl contains arithmetic infrastructure
  (zero_tangent_internal, _scale_internal, _dot_internal, etc.) and all
  conversion/math rules — using natural BFloat16 arithmetic without

* Guard bfloat16s test for platforms where BFloat16s.BFloat16 !== Core.BFloat16

On aarch64 with LLVM < 19, BFloat16s.jl defines its own primitive type and
Core.BFloat16 has no arithmetic. Tests are skipped there and run on x86_64
(LLVM >= 15) where BFloat16s.BFloat16 === Core.BFloat16.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use #! format: off in bfloat16.jl to avoid indentation inside @static if

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix BFloat16 extension rules and tests

- Fix sin/cos frules/rrules: use separate sin(x)+cos(x) calls instead of
  sincos(x) which infinitely recurses in Julia 1.12 for BFloat16
- Fix sinpi/cospi frules/rrules: use sinpi(x)/cospi(x) directly instead of
  sincospi(x) which returns Float64 and mismatches primal for edge cases
- Add Base.sinpi/cospi overloads for BFloat16 returning correct BFloat16 values
- Add Base.atan(y::P, x::P) overload since two-arg atan is not defined for
  BFloat16 in Julia 1.12
- Add JET and AllocCheck to test deps (required by test_tangent_interface)
- Use FD-friendly test values in [0.25, 0.5) BFloat16 range where finite
  differences are accurate given BFloat16's limited precision
- Add atol=0.5, rtol=0.5 tolerances to test_rule calls to accommodate
  BFloat16 quantization artifacts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Drop sinpi, cospi, and two-arg atan rules and tests

These functions have broken or inconsistent behaviour for BFloat16 inputs
in Julia 1.12 (sinpi/cospi return Float64, atan(::BF16,::BF16) is missing),
so the rules and their test cases are removed rather than worked around.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Tighten BFloat16 test tolerances to atol=0.2, rtol=0.4

The previous atol=0.5, rtol=0.5 were overly loose. Analysis of BFloat16 FD
behaviour guides the new values:

- rtol=0.4 covers the ~24% relative snap error when test values are in the
  [0.125, 0.25) BFloat16 range (spacing 0.000977), where the ε=1e-2
  perturbation rounds to exactly one grid step (0.000977) vs the intended
  ~0.00129, giving ẋ_fd/ẋ ≈ 0.76.

- atol=0.2 covers two edge cases that cannot be fixed by value selection alone:
  · acos(P(0.2)): output ∈ [1,2) with BF16 spacing 0.0078, so ẏ_fd=0 at all
    ε<1e-2; at ε=1e-2 the snap gives |LHS-RHS|=0.159 which needs atol≥0.16.
  · exp10(P(0.249)): chosen so f'(x)≈4.08 just exceeds the 4.0 threshold
    needed for the output perturbation to cross a BF16 boundary, giving a
    non-zero ẏ_fd at ε=1e-2 and |LHS-RHS|≈0.156 < atol=0.2.

All other test values moved to [0.125, 0.25) or [0.2, 0.25) to ensure:
1. Input perturbation is not absorbed (half-spacing 0.000488 < 0.00129)
2. Snap error stays within rtol=0.4
3. rrule tests use normalized tangents (|ẋ|≈1) so ε=1e-2 >> spacing, giving
   accurate FD well within atol=0.2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment explaining BFloat16 test tolerances

commit.message:
Create all Python backend files for video SaaS platform

- Core: custom exceptions, 16-state state machine, JWT/bcrypt/Fernet security
- Config: Pydantic Settings v2 with all env vars
- Database: SQLAlchemy 2.0 async engine (asyncpg) + Base + get_db
- Models: User, SocialAccount, ContentJob, JobAsset, JobHook, PublishTarget, PublishAttempt, AuditLog (UUID PKs, JSONB, enums)
- Schemas: Pydantic v2 schemas for auth, jobs, hooks, social, publishing
- API: FastAPI routers for auth, jobs wizard, social OAuth, publishing status
- Services: AuthService, JobService, HookService (OpenAI), StorageService (S3/boto3), SocialService (4 platforms)
- Workers: Celery tasks for video processing (FFmpeg), hook generation, publishing, beat scheduler
- Publishers: Abstract base + YouTube/TikTok/Instagram/Facebook stubs with detailed TODO comments
- Infra: Alembic async env.py, alembic.ini, script.py.mako, requirements.txt, multi-stage Dockerfile

commit.message:
fix(status): align agent rows by ljust-ing only the ASCII name

Emoji symbols have mismatched code-point counts vs terminal display widths
(e.g. 🛠️ = 2 code points, 📋/🎯 = 1 code point, but all render 2 cols).
Including the symbol in the ljust field caused the note column to be
misaligned by 1 space for coder rows.

Fix: separate symbol from agent name; apply ljust only to the pure-ASCII
name field so padding is always correct regardless of emoji encoding.

Before:
  📋 planner-1   ready to plan  (board empty)
  🛠️  coder-1    idle   (no work ready)
  🎯 qa-1        idle

After:
  📋 planner-1   ready to plan  (board empty)
  🛠️ coder-1     idle  (no work ready)
  🎯 qa-1        idle

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

(default 100ms). Clients can override via ?throttleMs= query param
on the /ws/sensors WebSocket, or --throttle on the CLI stream command.
Setting throttleMs=0 disables throttling for full-speed capture.

commit.message:
fix: rewrite /etc/hosts via inode overwrite to bypass bind mount rename restriction

Use 'cat > /etc/hosts' instead of 'sed -i' to patch BuildKit container
hosts file. sed -i fails with 'Resource busy' on Docker bind mounts because
it tries to rename a temp file over the original. Writing via cat preserves
the inode and avoids the restriction.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
